title: unstudio
tagline: Spatial intervention at Harvard GSD — a collision space for 350+ students without studio

what.content: A pilot collaborative space for the third of Harvard GSD students who had no home base in Gund Hall. The [unstudio](unstudiogsd.org) transformed an underutilized room into a drop-in collision space using furniture that already existed — where students could spontaneously meet, share ideas, increase interdisciplinary connections, and design collaboratively regardless of program.

The results were striking: The unstudio ran for just 10 days (April 11–20, 2026), yet hosted 7 events, and drew 163 total observed visits, doing computer work, drawing, modeling, and conversation simultaneously.

84 members of the community added their voice in support of the unstudio being made a permanent part of the school. Collaborations sprung out of it, design work was deepened, spontaneous feedback was shared. 

It cost nothing. It used furniture that already exists. It turned underutilized collaborative space into a resource for everyone.

why.content: Gund Hall was designed for 400 students yet in Spring 2026 held 994 — 2.5× over capacity.
Over 370 students have no desk in the school every semester.
Yet 89% of existing desks sit empty at any given time, and 624 private desks occupy the public space.

The cultural belief that "desks were only for studio students" was excluding a third of the school from their creative home base. The unstudio tested a different idea: what if shared space could belong to everyone?

how.content: Led with data: walked the space and sampled usage every 4 hours for a week before making any ask. Pitched the proposal to decision-makers framed as a low-risk, temporary pilot — doing all the homework so they only had to say yes.
Rearranged existing furniture into a flexible, drop-in layout. Posted three shared norms on the wall: Bring anything. Share everything. / Give up space. Don't hoard space. / Work with, not against.

Activation posters gave users an immediate, low-stakes way to participate — a message board, a cat mood chart, a birthday calendar, a shared space survey. Measured everything: visitor counts, activities, time of day, and a community reaction wall. The toolkit is free to download and remix for any institution.

description: The unstudio is a pilot for a collaborative learning space that solves the Harvard Graduate School of Design's spatial constraints.
scraped_raw_text: The unstudio is a pilot for a collaborative learning space that solves the Harvard Graduate School of Design's spatial constraints.

Every semester, over a third of GSD students across all programs (350+) have no home base in Gund Hall, the main building. The unstudio is a collision space for those students - where they can spontaneously meet, share ideas, increase interdisciplinary connections, design collaboratively, and learn more effectively in a community of practice.
